ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a tofu food product concept; silken tofu is a sub-type. A specific silken tofu (soft, steamed) concept would be more precise than the generic tofu concept.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of steamed silken tofu consists of half a block (approximately 150g) of smooth, custard-like soft tofu that is steamed until just heated through, and may be served plain or with a light soy sauce, sesame oil, and spring onion dressing.

nutrient_source.title: Silken Tofu — USDA Foundation Foods and HPB Singapore; USDA FDC ID 172448
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (Foundation Foods); cross-referenced with HPB Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Exact match found in USDA Foundation Foods for silken tofu (soft, raw/plain). Steamed preparation does not significantly alter macronutrient profile.

gi_source: University of Sydney GI Database — tofu (all types); GI is very low due to minimal carbohydrate content and the protein-fat composition.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium approximately 18mg per serving (150g) — less than 1% of the 2300mg daily recommended limit; silken tofu is one of the lowest-sodium protein foods available.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension may consume silken tofu freely; the isoflavones in soy have been shown to have a modest blood pressure-lowering effect, and the low sodium makes it an ideal protein food for hypertensive patients.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Extremely low GI (~15) and negligible glycaemic load; high protein content moderates blood glucose responses; soy isoflavones may improve insulin sensitivity.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Silken tofu is one of the most diabetes-friendly protein foods available; its extremely low GI, high protein, and low carbohydrate content make it an ideal component of a diabetic meal plan.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Soy isoflavones have demonstrated anti-inflammatory effects in rheumatoid arthritis research; high calcium and magnesium support bone health which is compromised in RA; completely plant-based.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Silken tofu is an excellent food for patients with rheumatoid arthritis; the soy isoflavones provide anti-inflammatory benefit, the calcium supports bone health, and it is a low-inflammatory-load protein alternative to meat.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Silken tofu is the softest variety of tofu — a delicate, smooth, custard-like soy protein product made from minimally processed whole soybean milk coagulated with gypsum (calcium sulfate), nigari, or GDL to form a smooth, fragile curd with very high water content. In Singapore, steamed silken tofu is typically dressed with a light sauce of soy, sesame oil, and spring onion or served in soups and stews. It is one of the most versatile, nutritious, and allergy-friendly protein foods available — high in protein, calcium, and soy isoflavones with negligible carbohydrate content.
common_user_queries: how many calories in silken tofu
is tofu good for weight loss
silken vs firm tofu which has more protein

confidence_reason: Exact match from USDA Foundation Foods for silken tofu (soft, plain). USDA data is the highest-fidelity source for this widely standardised product.
notes: Calcium content is notably high (111mg/100g) when coagulated with gypsum (calcium sulfate); tofu made with nigari (magnesium chloride) will have lower calcium and higher magnesium. Vegan and fully plant-based. Cholesterol and vitamin B12 are 0.0. The dressing (soy sauce, sesame oil) adds sodium and should be accounted for separately.
